description: Ordered verification gates. Run in order; stop at first failure. Code gates went 'active' with the F-001 scaffold. 'e2e' went 'active' with F-011 (the REST API — first user-facing surface). 'a11y' active since F-028 (ADR-0021). 'e2e-full' (NFR-16) went 'active' with F-048 — the first gate that exercises the product as ONE deployment rather than a slice. 'web-perf' now activates with F-049 (it was slated for F-028 and never activated — drift caught in the 2026-07-04 launch-readiness review); 'perf' (NFR-4) activates with F-049.

gates.description: Harness integrity gate (hardened 2026-07-04): state files (feature_list.json, effects.json, schema sync), workflow invariants (wip_limit, blockers-done before in_progress/done, plan-before-code for in-flight features), PRD requirement traceability, gates.json validity + CI-mirror (E-005), memory index coverage, governed-doc relative links, and the env-docs guard (every TESSERA_* var read by config/server documented in .env.example).
TypeScript strict typecheck across the workspace (turbo run typecheck).
ESLint (flat config) incl. package-boundary rule (turbo run lint).
Prettier formatting check (code + root config; docs/harness excluded).
Unit + integration tests via Vitest (turbo run test), incl. adapter conformance suites.
Turborepo build of all packages and apps.

End-to-end tests across surfaces. api/mcp run HTTP-/SDK-surface e2e (app.inject / in-memory transport; active since F-011). Web (since F-028) runs Playwright against its own dev server, including @axe-core WCAG A/AA assertions. CI installs the Playwright browser before this gate.

Accessibility (axe / WCAG 2.1 AA) for apps/web — active since F-028 (ADR-0021). Asserted inside the web Playwright e2e (gate 6); CI's e2e step covers it (ciStep:false — the verify-state ci-mirror guard knows this is deliberate). Listed here for traceability.

Frontend performance budget (bundle size / Lighthouse CI) for every web app (dashboard + marketing + docs). Planned (ADR-0021); ACTIVATES WITH F-049 (drift fix: originally 'activates with F-028' but was never turned on). Enforces code-split of heavy views, a lean initial bundle, and Core Web Vitals budgets on public surfaces (NFR-17).

Full-stack end-to-end (NFR-16) — ACTIVE since F-048; lives in tests/e2e-full (@tessera/e2e-full). Boots the REAL server via startApiServer (Local profile, file-backed SQLite in a temp dir, fake embeddings by default; real Transformers.js behind TESSERA_E2E_REAL_EMBEDDINGS=1), registers a fixture repo + scans it before reporting healthy, then runs two journeys against that ONE live deployment: the HUMAN journey (Playwright drives the real apps/web — production build, proxied at the live API, not its own stub — sign-in → sources → search → inspector → capture a memory in the real Monaco editor → audit) and the AGENT journey (a real MCP client spawns the real tessera-mcp binary over stdio against the same database, exercising search/compile_context/get_effects/capture_memory/add_source with budget-bounded + token-lean assertions, cross-verified through REST). Serialized (workers:1) and retries:0 — a flaky full-stack gate is a lie. Ports 3200/3201 so it can run back-to-back with the e2e gate.

Backend performance benchmarks (NFR-4) — ACTIVE since F-049; lives in tests/bench (@tessera/bench). Boots the REAL Local runtime in-process over a VERSIONED, deterministic corpus (seeded generator + pinned mtimes — content alone is not enough, since the temporal retriever ranks on mtime and rank-based fusion then shuffles the result set) and asserts committed thresholds (tests/bench/thresholds.json): search p95 < 300 ms and compile p95 < 2 s at the default budget (both NFR-4), incremental-ingest p95, tokens-per-answer for REST *and* MCP separately (ADR-0036 parity), the compile envelope ratio (response tokens / package tokens), and FR-30's budget promise. Thresholds are ABSOLUTE, never baseline deltas: CI hardware varies, so a delta gate would flake until it was disabled. Fake embeddings by default — it measures OUR engine, stays machine-comparable, and keeps the NFR-4 headroom that makes the gate stable; the real-provider number is measured under TESSERA_BENCH_REAL_EMBEDDINGS=1 and recorded in results/baseline.json (never gated on). A miss is a tuning work item, never an edited threshold.
